title: Decimals on the number line: hundredths
description: Practice finding decimal numbers on the number line. Decimals are limited to hundredths.
topic_path: Decimals on the number line: hundredths

assessment_items.question_content:
**Where is the point on the number line?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]



[[☃ image 1]]
hints: The length between the blue labeled tick marks is $\blueD{0.1}$, or $\blueD{1 \text{ tenth}}$.
Each $\blueD{0.1}$ is divided into $10$ equal pieces that have a length of $\goldD{0.01}$, or $\goldD{1 \text{ hundredth}}$.

The green point is $\goldD{5 \text{ hundredths}}$ after $0.1$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$0.1$ and $\goldD{ \text{5 hundredths}}$ equals ${0.1\goldD5}$. 

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | $\goldD{\text{Hundredths}}$
:-: | :-: |:-: |:-: 
$0$ | $.$ | $1$  | $\goldD5$



[[☃ image 1]]

The answer:

$\greenD{0.15}$

options.alt: A number line labeled 0 to 0.2  with tick marks at every 0.01 unit. Tick marks 0, 0.1, and 0.2 are labeled in blue. A green dot labeled with a question mark is on the number line at the fifth tick mark after 0.1.

Which number line graphs a point $\goldD{ \text{4 hundredths}}$ to the right of $3.7$?



[[☃ image 1]]

The following number line graphs $3.74$:
